title: numpy>=2.0 incompatible with CANN 8.x ACL initialization
problem_description: numpy 2.x introduces ABI changes that break CANN 8.x telemetry and ACL initialization. The default pip install resolves to numpy 2.2.6, which causes ACL init failure when torch-npu attempts to communicate with the Ascend driver. Must pin numpy<2.0.
rough_fix_approach: 1. After detecting CANN 8.x on the host, add numpy<2.0 as a constraint. 2. Install numpy 1.26.x explicitly before or alongside torch-npu. 3. Verify ACL initialization succeeds via `python -c 'import torch_npu; print(torch_npu.npu.device_count())'`. 4. If ACL init fails with numpy 2.x, downgrade to 1.26.x.
artifact_evidence: fix_code summary field references numpy pinning
reasoning: numpy 2.0 ABI incompatibility with CANN is a known systemic issue affecting all NPU projects. The fix is a simple version pin, but the symptom (ACL init failure) is cryptic and hard to debug. Proactively constraining numpy during venv setup prevents a common failure mode.
